highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.218 Released
highlights.summary: The latest release moves `/code-review` to run as a background subagent, meaning code review work no longer clutters your main conversation thread. This also fixes a key behavior where stacked slash commands now correctly use the review target. Screen-reader accessibility is improved with announcements for deleted text.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.216 Released
highlights.summary: This release fixes a significant performance bug where long sessions experienced multi-second stalls due to message normalization costs growing quadratically with the number of turns — if your sessions felt sluggish, this update should help. A new `sandbox.filesystem.disabled` setting lets you skip filesystem isolation while still keeping network egress controls active.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.214 Released — Permission & Security Fixes
highlights.summary: This release patches two important security issues: a permission-check bypass affecting Windows PowerShell 5.1 users, and a bug where `Edit(src/**)` allow rules incorrectly auto-approved writes to nested directories anywhere in the file tree instead of only within your working directory. If you use Claude Code on Windows or rely on scoped edit permissions, updating immediately is strongly recommended.

highlights.title: Claude Code: Anatomy of a Misfeature
highlights.summary: This opinion piece critically examines a specific Claude Code behavior that the author argues causes more harm than good, making it a useful read for developers who want to think critically about how AI coding assistants can subtly degrade workflow quality. Understanding what makes a feature a 'misfeature' helps you configure and use Claude Code more intentionally.

highlights.title: Setting Up a Spare Mac for Claude Code to Control — Step-by-Step Guide
highlights.summary: This guide walks you through configuring a spare Mac as a dedicated machine for Claude Code to operate autonomously, enabling more powerful agentic workflows without risking your main development environment. It's a practical approach for developers who want to experiment with Claude Code's computer-use capabilities safely.

highlights.title: Cognikernel — Local Memory for AI Coding Assistants
highlights.summary: Cognikernel is an open-source tool that gives Claude Code persistent memory across sessions, storing architectural decisions and context that would otherwise be lost when a session ends. Rather than manually maintaining a `CLAUDE.md` file, Cognikernel automates the memory layer so your AI assistant remembers past decisions.

highlights.title: Claude Code Tutorial for Non-Coders
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial is designed specifically for people with no coding background who want to start using Claude Code to build software through natural language. It covers the basics of getting started, making it an ideal first watch if you're completely new to AI-assisted development.

highlights.title: 9 Minutes to Get Started: Claude Code Installation, Concepts & Real Usage (China Direct Access)
highlights.summary: This Bilibili video offers a concise beginner-friendly guide to installing Claude Code and understanding how it works, including tips for users in China who need direct connectivity solutions. In just nine minutes it covers setup, core concepts, and practical real-world usage examples.

tips_of_the_day: Use `/code-review` as an explicit slash command rather than expecting Claude to trigger it automatically — since v2.1.215 it only runs on demand, giving you full control over when reviews happen and keeping your conversation clean.

If you rely on `Edit(src/**)` or similar glob allow rules in your permissions config, double-check your patterns after updating to v2.1.214: rules now correctly scope to your current working directory only, so overly broad patterns may need to be updated to match your intended directories.

Instead of rewriting your `CLAUDE.md` after every major session, consider keeping a running 'decisions log' section at the bottom where you append key architectural choices with dates — this gives Claude Code rolling context without you having to restructure the whole file.
